front: Poem: addresses the title city as 'Hog Butcher for the World, Tool Maker, Stacker of Wheat' and 'City of the Big Shoulders'
Work: six-volume biography divided into The Prairie Years and The War Years; won the 1940 Pulitzer for History; inspired Sherwood's Abe Lincoln in Illinois
Poem: grass speaks, commanding 'Pile the bodies high at Austerlitz and Waterloo / shovel them under and let me work'
Poem: title phenomenon comes 'on little cat feet' and 'sits looking over harbor and city on silent haunches'
Author: compiled the American Songbag folk collection; wrote the prologue to The Family of Man photography exhibition
